worksForYou.title: Works for you, grows with you
worksForYou.sub: Memory and personalization under your control.

skills.title: Security Architecture
skills.desc: CoPaw 1.0 adopts a three-layer defense architecture—Tool Guard, File Guard, and Skill Scanner—to protect against runtime threats, unauthorized file access, and risky skill installations across all agent operations.

control.title: Multi-agent Workspaces
control.desc: CoPaw 1.0 enables multiple isolated agent workspaces to run concurrently within a single instance, each with independent configurations and memory, supporting asynchronous collaboration for complex multi-role tasks.

apps.title: CoPaw Custom Models
apps.desc: CoPaw 1.0 introduces the CoPaw-Flash series—purpose-built small models for local deployment that require no cloud dependency, offer one-click setup, and are specifically optimized for high-frequency tasks such as document processing, memory update, and information retrieval.

memory.title: Memory
memory.desc: Compress context and tool output; turn experience into reusable memory—personalization stays under your control.

toolkit.title: Tools, Skills & MCP
toolkit.desc: 20+ built-in tools and skills with hot reload; plug in MCP and add skills anytime—your toolkit grows with CoPaw.

clientVoices.title: Listen to what the community says about CoPaw
clientVoices.sub: See real feedback from CoPaw users

t1.text: CoPaw is a truly out-of-the-box, general-purpose AI agent framework. As CoPaw's underlying capabilities continue to iterate, our self-developed business agents can also achieve a leap in intelligence, significantly reducing long-term maintenance costs and technical liabilities.

t2.text: Alibaba Tongyi’s move this time is very solid. CoPaw strengthens the “local + cloud” dual-deployment model, which is highly practical in today’s environment where privacy sensitivity and computing demands coexist. The open-source strategy also reflects Alibaba’s openness in building its AI ecosystem.

t3.text: It's great, very easy to use, and responds quickly when used with Qwen's models. I suggest you consider using it on embedded devices to reduce resource consumption.

t4.text: The code looks so clean and easy to understand. Being a developer myself, would love to play with it more.

t5.text: MCP hot-swapping without restarting is the standout feature for agent workflows. That means you can extend CoPaw's toolset mid-session, no context break, no restart penalty. Skill Hub + Claw Hub integration means the ecosystem compounds fast.

t6.text: The CoPaw open-source project from Tongyi Lab is truly impressive! It’s great to see that people are not only enjoying successful experiences while using it, but also sharing amusing mishaps—like trying to tidy up the desktop and accidentally deleting files. Open-source AI agent frameworks are extremely important for advancing the entire industry, and I look forward to seeing more innovative applications built on CoPaw.

brandstory.title: Why CoPaw?
brandstory.para1: CoPaw represents both a Co Personal Agent Workstation and a "co-paw"—a partner always by your side.
brandstory.para2: More than just a cold tool, CoPaw is a warm "little paw" always ready to lend a hand (or a paw!). It is the ultimate teammate for your digital life.

channels.title: Every channel
channels.desc: DingTalk, Feishu, QQ, Discord, iMessage, and more — one assistant, connect as you need.

private.title: Under your control
private.desc: Memory and personalization under your control. Deploy locally or in the cloud; scheduled reminders and collaboration to any channel.

skills.title: Skills
skills.desc: Built-in Cron; custom skills in your workspace, auto-loaded.

testimonials.title: What people say
testimonials.1: This is what a personal assistant should be: one entry, every channel.
testimonials.2: Cron and heartbeat are super practical. Add your own skills; data stays local.
testimonials.3: Teams who want full control love it.

usecases.title: What you can do with CoPaw
usecases.sub: Memory and personalization under your control.

social.title: Social Media
social.description: CoPaw helps you stay on top of social content by summarizing trending posts, new videos, and account patterns based on your interests.

creative.title: Creative & building
creative.description: CoPaw can run overnight on your goals and support the entire content creation process from idea to draft.

productivity.title: Productivity
productivity.description: CoPaw boosts productivity by summarizing updates, organizing contacts, and analyzing personal logs for useful patterns.

research.title: Research & learning
research.description: CoPaw tracks important company updates and builds your personal knowledge base for easy retrieval and reuse.

homeFaq.title: Frequently asked questions
homeFaq.sub: Memory and personalization under your control.
homeFaq.intro: Here's everything you need to know to get started, manage your account, and troubleshoot the most frequent issues.

install.p1: CoPaw supports multiple installation methods, including:
install.li1: Desktop Application (Beta): download and run the desktop app with no command line required, suitable for users unfamiliar with terminals.
install.li2: Script install: run on your machine with no Python setup required.
install.li3: pip install: if you prefer managing Python yourself.
install.li4: Docker: use official images from Docker Hub (ACR is also available for users in China).
install.li5: ModelScope Studio: one-click cloud deployment, no local install needed.
install.li6: Alibaba Cloud ECS: one-click deployment on Alibaba Cloud, no local install.

update.p1: To update CoPaw, use the method matching your installation type:
update.li1: If installed via one-line script, re-run the installer to upgrade.
update.li3: If installed from source, pull the latest code and reinstall.
update.li4: If using Docker, pull the latest image and restart the container.
update.li5Prefix: If using the Windows Desktop App (exe), currently you need to uninstall and reinstall:

initialize.q: How to initialize and start CoPaw service
initialize.p3Suffix: . After quick init, you can open Console and customize settings.

upgrade.q: Where to check latest version upgrade details
upgrade.p1: You can check version changes in the Change Log section of Console, or on the CoPaw

jobs.a: The easiest way to create a cron job is to ask CoPaw directly in your target channel. For example: Create a scheduled task that reminds me to drink water every five minutes." You can also create and manage cron jobs manually in Console → Control → Cron Jobs.

models.li1: Cloud models: enter the provider API key (e.g. ModelScope, DashScope, or a custom provider).
models.p2: After configuration, choose the target provider and model under Default LLM at the top of the Models page and Save — that becomes the global default.
models.p3: To use a different model per agent, switch the agent with the selector at the top-left of Console, then pick a model in the top-right of the Chat page for that agent.
models.p4Mid: for setup, downloads, and switching. See
models.cliLink: CLI → Models and environment variables → copaw models

skills.p1Mid: in Console. You can enable/disable Skills, create custom Skills, and import Skills from Skills Hub. See

mcp.p1Mid: in Console. You can enable/disable/delete/create MCP clients there. See

error401.p1: If you encounter the following error:
error401.err: Error code: 401 - {'error': {'message': "You didn't provide an API key. You need to provide your API key in an Authorization header using Bearer auth (i.e. Authorization: Bearer YOUR_KEY)."}}
error401.p2: This is typically caused by one of the following:
error401.li1: No API key is configured. Get an API key and configure it in Console → Settings → Models.
error401.li2Prefix: An API key is configured but authentication still fails. In most cases, one of the configuration fields is incorrect. Check the following:
error401.li2a: Base URL — Make sure it is correct.
error401.li2b: API key — Make sure it is copied in full, with no extra spaces.
error401.li2c: Model name — Make sure it exactly matches the provider's value (case-sensitive).

localModels.p1: In most cases, this is not a CoPaw bug. The root cause is usually that the model's context length is configured too small.
localModels.p2: When you deploy a local model with Ollama or LM Studio, if the model's context length is too low, CoPaw may show problems such as:
localModels.li1: failing to sustain multi-turn conversations reliably
localModels.li2: losing context during complex tool calls
localModels.li3: forgetting instructions given in earlier turns
localModels.li4: drifting away from the task during long-running interactions
localModels.fix1: Before running CoPaw, set the model's context length to at least 32K.
localModels.fix2: For more complex tasks, frequent tool calls, or longer conversations, you may need a value higher than 32K.

cron.p1: If a scheduled task doesn't run as expected, try the following steps:
cron.s1: Confirm that the CoPaw service is running.
cron.s2: In Console, go to Control → Cron Jobs and ensure the task Status is set to Enabled.
cron.s3: On the same page, confirm that Dispatch Channel is set to your intended channel (e.g., Console, DingTalk, Feishu, Discord, iMessage).
cron.s4: Also confirm that Dispatch Target User ID and Dispatch Target Session ID are correctly configured.
cron.s5: In Console, go to Control → Sessions and locate the session used when creating the task. The User ID and Session ID there must match the task's Dispatch Target User ID and Dispatch Target Session ID for replies to appear in that session.
cron.s6: If the task runs at the wrong time, go back to Control → Cron Jobs and verify the Schedule (Cron) expression.
cron.s7: To manually test the task, click Execute Now on the Cron Jobs page. If successful, the reply will appear in the target channel. Alternatively, you can ask CoPaw directly: "Trigger the 'drink water reminder' task I just created."

help.p1: If you need help, you can:
help.s2Mid: with the full error message, the error detail file (e.g.
help.s2Suffix: ), your model provider, model name, and CoPaw version.
